title: Adopt a Mitigated Negative Declaration for the Tom Bates Regional Sports Complex Restroom and Community Space Project

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution adopting the Mitigated Negative Declaration (MND) and Mitigation
Monitoring and Reporting Plan (MMRP) for the Tom Bates Regional Sports Complex
Restroom and Community Space Project.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
There are no direct fiscal impacts associated with the adoption of mitigated negative
declaration environmental documents. The Mitigated Negative Declaration for the Tom
Bates Regional Sports Complex Restroom and Community Space Project was prepared
by TranSystems, Inc. under contract number 32000263. Funding for the engineering
and environmental consulting services from TranSystems, Inc. was appropriated in FY
2023 Measure T1, Phase 2 (Fund 511).
sections.current_situation:
In August of 2022, the City retained TranSystems, Inc. to prepare the Initial Study /
Mitigated Negative Declaration (IS/MND) and Mitigation Monitoring and Reporting Plan
(MMRP) environmental documents to assess the environmental impacts of the
proposed restrooms and community space improvements at the Tom Bates Regional
Sports Complex facility as required by the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA).
The MND determined that the project will have less than significant environmental
impacts if mitigation measures identified in the MND are implemented. The specific
mitigation measures are shown in the attached Final Initial Study / Mitigated Negative
Declaration and Mitigation Monitoring and Reporting Plan.
On November 6, 2024, the Draft MND was distributed to the State Clearinghouse, all
regulatory agencies having jurisdiction over the Project, interested stakeholders and
members of the public for the required 30-day public review period ending on December
7, 2024, as required by CEQA. On November 13, 2024, the MND was presented at the
regular meeting of the Parks, Recreation and Waterfront Commission. During the 30-
day public review period, a total of 42 written comment letters from both agencies and
members of the public were received. The Draft MND published on State Clearinghouse

On November 6, 2024, the City also recorded the Draft IS/MND at the Alameda County

sections.background:
The Tom Bates Regional Sports Complex, located at 400 Gilman Street was completed
in 2008, and is one of the most highly used public sports complexes in the region. The
master plan for the Tom Bates Fields includes a fieldhouse and restroom; however,
these elements have not been constructed-to-date due to insufficient funding, and
therefore the City currently provides portable toilets at the facility. In 2019, the City
completed the conceptual plan for the new fieldhouse and restroom in collaboration with
the field user groups and the five Joint Powers Agreement cities (Albany, Berkeley, El
Cerrito, Emeryville, and Richmond), using Measure T1 infrastructure bond funding. The
project will also convert the vacant north field into a youth soccer field, with a plaza and
stairway entrance, and six pickleball courts. In August of 2022, the City retained
TranSystems, Inc. to prepare final design including construction documents, and
prepare applicable permitting and CEQA environmental documents.
The total Project budget estimate is approximately $8 Million, which includes planning,
design, permitting, environmental review, and construction. The Project is being funded
by Measure T1 Phase 2, General Fund and Parks Tax. The City currently anticipates
beginning construction in the Fall of 2025, with a goal to complete construction by end
of 2026.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The Mitigated Negative Declaration for the proposed project contains a comprehensive
environmental assessment of the project. The assessment determined that the Project
will have a less-than significant impact on the environment if the City implements
specific mitigation measures. The Mitigation, Monitoring and Reporting Plan includes
detailed roles, responsibilities, and methods for implementing and documenting
compliance with the mitigation measures.
sections.rationale:
The adoption of the Mitigated Negative Declaration for the proposed Tom Bates
Regional Sports Complex Restroom and Community Space Project will allow the City to
move into the bidding phase of the Project in May, 2025. The approval of project and
award of construction contract will take place at a Council Meeting in July, 2025.

ADOPTING MITIGATED NEGATIVE DECLARATION (MND) AND MITIGATION

SPORTS COMPLEX RESTROOM AND COMMUNITY SPACE PROJECT
WHEREAS, the Tom Bates Regional Sports Complex, located at 400 Gilman Street
was completed in 2008, and is one of the most highly used public sports complexes in
the region; and
WHEREAS, the master plan for the Tom Bates Fields includes a fieldhouse and
restroom; however, these elements have not been constructed to-date due to
insufficient funding, and the City currently provides portable toilets at the facility; and
WHEREAS, in 2019, the City completed the conceptual plan for a new fieldhouse and
restroom in collaboration with the field user groups and the five Joint Powers Agreement
cities (Albany, Berkeley, El Cerrito, Emeryville, and Richmond); and
WHEREAS, the project will also convert the vacant north field into a youth soccer field,
with a plaza and stairway entrance, and six pickleball courts, and
WHEREAS, the City retained Transystems, Inc. to prepare an Initial Study / Mitigated
Negative Declaration environmental documents and associated Mitigation Monitoring
and Reporting Plan to assess the environmental impacts of the proposed Tom Bates
Regional Sports Complex Restroom and Community Space Project as required by the
California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA); and
WHEREAS, on November 6, 2024, the City issued a Notice of Intent to Adopt a
Mitigated Negative Declaration; and
WHEREAS, on November 6, 2024, the IS/MND was distributed to responsible agencies
and the public for review and comment for a 30-day period that ended December 7,
